commands.name: /swarm
commands.summary: Master conductor that selects between commands, skills, and orchestrators.
notes: Default entrypoint when the best command is unclear.
Should bias toward multi-agent and parallel routing for nontrivial tasks.

commands.name: /hitit
commands.summary: Main execution entrypoint for doing the work.
notes: Can internally hand off to planning or research before execution.
Parallel execution is preferred when subtasks are independent.

commands.name: /doublecheck
commands.summary: Validation, correctness, tests, config, quality, and findings-first review.
notes: Primary review command; output should prioritize issues over summary.
Should use multiple internal validation paths whenever possible.

commands.name: /doubt
commands.summary: Targeted challenge to the current approach with explicit alternatives.
notes: Focuses on the current frame rather than rebuilding from scratch.
Should compare the active direction against at least one viable alternative.

commands.name: /plan
commands.summary: Architecture, sequencing, standards, modularity, and implementation strategy.
notes: Should produce modular, standards-first implementation plans.

commands.name: /enhance
commands.summary: Pull in related repos, conventions, datasets, APIs, and prior art.
notes: Should distinguish strong conventions from interesting-but-niche patterns.
Should favor multiple sources in parallel for nontrivial discovery.

commands.name: /humanize
commands.summary: Improve prose, UX wording, documentation tone, readability, and polish.
notes: Bias toward clarity, natural phrasing, and lower-friction UX language.

commands.name: /docs
commands.summary: Generate missing documentation and upgrade existing README, guides, and reference docs.
notes: Owns end-to-end documentation generation.
Can call humanize internally for polish.

commands.name: /janitor
commands.summary: Cleanup, organization, dead artifact detection, repo hygiene, and cleanup-oriented snippet capture.
notes: Cleanup-oriented snippet capture is allowed only when tied to cleanup work.

commands.name: /pushit
commands.summary: Repo publication, sync, packaging publish, and distribution steps.
notes: Owns repository and package publication, not deployment.

commands.name: /shipit
commands.summary: Release, deploy, preflight, postflight, and verification.
notes: Owns deployment and release safety checks.

commands.name: /extract
commands.summary: Pitch a product plan, business plan, audience, and monetization strategy for existing code.
notes: Treats the current codebase as raw product material.
Should answer who it is for, why it matters, and how it could make money.

commands.name: /portfolio
commands.summary: Determine where code could be submitted, showcased, or distributed for visibility and credibility.
notes: Includes awesome lists, showcases, CodePen-style venues, galleries, and repo directories.
Should rank venues by fit rather than dumping a flat list.

commands.name: /harvest
commands.summary: Capture reusable snippets, implementation patterns, and durable ideas.
notes: Separate from cleanup; preserves useful patterns instead of deleting them.

commands.name: /thinkagain
commands.summary: Reconsider the whole implementation from first principles.
notes: Use when the current approach may be wrong at a foundational level.

commands.name: /consensus
commands.summary: Read-only second opinions from other installed CLI agents.
notes: External agents are consulted in read-only mode only.
